segments.text: So now before we get into the demo, I just want to share with you a quick overview of each tool.
So first up is the OpenArt AI. So OpenArt is a browser-based creative hub giving you access to
over 100 AI models, including Flux 1.1, Nano Banana 2, Clang, and more, all under one subscription.
It also covers image generation, video creation, character consistency, custom model training,
story-telling workflows, and audio. It is built for creators who want a full creative workspace
rather than a single-purpose tool. Now on the other hand, Higgs Field is a video-first platform
built around cinematic quality and camera control, which is why their dashboard is more on videos
rather than photos, because they are more aggressive towards creating videos. It aggregates
premium video models like Soro 2, Clang 3.0, Google View 3.1 under one interface, and wraps them in
professional cinematography tools, including Cinema Studio, with over 70 camera presets and
characters. So it's a great tool to use for creating videos, and it's a great tool to use
for creating videos. Now it is built for creators who want director-level control over their video
output. Synthesia is an AI avatar video platform built specifically for professional and corporate
use. You just have to write a script, pick from over 240 lifelike AI avatars, and the platform
generates a polished talking head video in over 140 languages. It is built for businesses, L&D
teams, HR departments, and marketers who need consistent professional presenter-style video
content at a scale.
Alright, let's start with Open Art AI. So what we're going to do is you're just going to head over to
openart.ai, and then from your dashboard, you're going to head over to the left side panel. You will
see this create section. So you can just click on video right here, but if you're on your dashboard,
you can also find the video right here. And then you're just going to have to choose whether you
want to do frame-to-video, text-to-video, edit-to-video, and all of these stuff. But for this
tutorial, we are going to use this text-to-video. Now, before we get started, let's go ahead and
see what we're going to do. So before typing anything, we're going to look at the model
selector right here at the top of the prompt box. And then from the model right here, you can see
all of the models that they have here available, all in the same platform. So let's talk about the
good ones first. So you can see the Seedance. This is actually their newest model, wherein you
can do cinematic videos with audio and multi-shots. And then you also have this Kling 3.0 Omni,
So you can find all of these new tags with the fast tags as well. These are usually the most
good or the best ones where you could produce videos because you can see that you can produce
cinematic videos with them. And then you can control it the way you want to. And then you
also have here the control of how many seconds you can generate with it. And if you're trying
to produce a 4K type of video, I think I would recommend Vue 3.1 because this one actually
produces high fidelity videos with audio and 4K. But for this tutorial, we are just going to use a
much cheaper one. So let's just use this Vue 3.1. This one is also new. And like I said,
it produces 4K videos. So we're just going to click on it. And then you can already see how
much credits we're going to have to spend in order to create a video out of Vue 3.1. Now,
what you're going to do next is you just have to describe the action in detail. You can also
add reference images to generate video, but consistent subjects. So as you can see, I already
have typed in the prompt that I want to use the main character preparing breakfast early in the
morning. Now you can find all of the tools right here if you just scroll down. So you can find the
audio right here. So if you want to produce a video with an audio, you can definitely just
toggle this on. And then you can also choose the mode right here if you want it to be light,
fast, or if you want to have a better quality than
choose quality right here. And then you can also choose the output type. So you can do vertical
videos, or if you're going to post it on YouTube, then you can do 16 by 9. The resolution as well,
you can choose whether you want it to be in 720p or 180p. Take note that in every tool that you or
like every feature that you alter, your credits will either increase or decrease. So as you can
see here, since we're producing a video in only 720p resolution,
you are using much less credits. So what you're going to do next is you're just going to click on
generate right here, and then we're going to wait for the video to be generated. And now we can see
the generated video right here. So you can also hear the sound of it. It's very crisp. And the
character also looks very realistic. It literally just looks like it came out from a movie. Now in
order for you to edit this, if you want to edit some of the components of the video, you can just
click on modify video right here. And then you can see that the video is generated. So you can
then you can choose a video to edit, which is this one, you can describe how you want to edit the
video. For example, you want to, like change some text, or you want to add some text images or
elements or any of the instructions that you want. And you can also have the audio turned off
if you want, you can change the output. And of course, you can change the model right here. So
for example, the model that you previously chose doesn't really satisfy you, then you can change
it. And then you can also upload a reference video right here if you want. And yeah, that's
basically how you're going to edit it. I think that this tool is actually very, very helpful,
especially with most AI is not offering this feature, like being able to just edit or like,
you know, add more instructions or like modify some of the elements in the video. I think it's
very helpful that you don't have to redo the prompt once again, or like adjust it every time
you try to edit some components.
I think on the creative workspace side, open art is in category of its own among these five tools,
the model variety that we have right here, like the several types of models that we can choose
from. It's very, it's very good in terms of variety, the editing layer as well. And then
there's also a character consistency system within this app. And you know what, video isn't the only
thing that you can do with it, you can see that we have more options, like we can create videos,
images, world, audio, as well.
As well as character. And aside from that, it gives you a level of creative control that none
of the others come close to matching. So if you need a full video or like text to video creative
workflow, this is definitely where you should come. And now that we're done with open art AI,
let's now move over to Higgs field AI. So from here, we are just going to test out its strongest
feature, which is cinema studio. Now what you're going to do is you're just going to head over to
the video tab right here at the top, you can just hover your mouse over it. And then you're going
to click on cinema studio video right here. Now you can also opt to generate AI videos by clicking on
create video, but I suggest that you use the cinema studio video feature. So we're just going to click
on that. Now from here, you will immediately notice what sets this platform apart with this
director panel. So from here, you can already see that you have the options for movement, you have
their auto, static, handheld, zoom out, zoom in, and all of the stuff that you can find right here
that you can use. So you can already see that you have the options for movement, you have the
auto, static, handheld, zoom in, and all of the stuff that you can find right here. So you can
also do crane shots, crash zooms, dolly moves, rack focus. And you can also see here that you have
this single shots, multi shot audio, multi shot manual, depending on what you need. And you can
also have the option here to set the aspect aspect ratio. So you have the option to set it to
cinematic. And then also if you're maybe doing this for TikTok, you can set it to nine by 16.
And then you have here the genre. So you can choose whether you want it to be a general genre,
it really depends on what you're going to do.
